blocks.content: The spot where the light had struck was sunken in a broad, flat depression.
Without a moment's hesitation, the two of them rushed over to examine the traces.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Wh-what is this? Magic? Or real sorcery?

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Strange. To imbue light with impact… I thought photon-based fusion was impossible.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone, what's going on?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's a new weapon I've mastered this time. Not photon output—it's a photon cannon.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: A photon cannon. Sounds impressive. What's the principle behind it?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Well… explaining it would take a while.
As Shirone hesitated, Neid hurried over, his face flushed with excitement, and shook Shirone's shoulders.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Out with it! How did you do it?! Embedding physical force into light—this is absolutely impossible!
Light and electricity were cousins, so Neid understood exactly what Shirone had accomplished.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'll tell you. But first… how about you come somewhere with me?
Shirone's group entered a restaurant in the noble district.
While filling their stomachs with seafood pasta, Shirone recounted how he had come to master the photon cannon.
The whirlwind of dust that had appeared over the bed, the moment when Ulk was thrown off the cliff, and the realization of the photon cannon's principle in that instant.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: I've heard of gauge symmetry from my father. The gist is this: there's a separate particle that conveys mass, right? And you've learned to manipulate that particle.
Shirone was once again reminded of how incredible Iruki's father was.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: So, in short, the result of the immortal function is mass. That's why your supernatural senses disappeared after mastering the photon cannon.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yeah. And I don't have nightmares anymore.
Iruki and Neid fell into thought.
A particle that conveys mass to matter—Shirone's realization was a monumental discovery.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: This is amazing, Shirone. This might be far more significant than we imagine. If you patent it, you could be sitting on a fortune right now!
Neid's mind was already buzzing with countless inventions that could be derived from Shirone's theory.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That's why I have a favor to ask. Would you come to the library with me?

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Huh? The library?

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: The school library is off-limits because of my disciplinary action. So I'm thinking of going to the noble library. I want to research the photon cannon further. But I can't get in alone, so I need you guys to take me.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That's no problem. It'll help us too. But why can't you go alone?

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Commoners weren't allowed in the noble library. Once his suspension was lifted, he could use the school library, but after his trip to the firefly village, he'd realized something. Denying his identity wouldn't change anything. At the very least, he wanted to be honest with his friends.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Actually, I'm not a noble.
Neid, who had been chewing pasta, froze with his mouth open.
Iruki also seemed shocked, staring intently at Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'm a commoner. I was abandoned in a stable as a child and raised by my current parents. My father is just an ordinary mountain hunter.
Though he'd entered the world of magic late, no one would've guessed he was a commoner.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: It's shocking, honestly. But I don't really feel any different. You're still Shirone, aren't you?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Same here. If I'd known from the start, maybe it'd be different, but hearing it now doesn't change anything. The time we've spent together doesn't just disappear.
Shirone had hoped his friends would understand, but that was just a personal expectation.
Hearing their acceptance made his heart swell.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: So that's why you were a special case. Learning magic late because you weren't a noble. Well, talent isn't something you're born with based on lineage. That's really impressive.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Thank you. For saying that.
Seeing Shirone's moved expression, Neid waved his hands dismissively.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: Hey, hey. No need to be so serious. I'm actually relieved. I'm glad I became friends with you without any preconceptions. If I'd known earlier, something like the research presentation a few days ago would've been impossible.
Iruki shook off his thoughts and stood up from the table.
Dragging this out would only dampen the mood.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Alright, let's head to the library. I was bored to death of lazing around anyway—this is perfect. Let's ignite our academic passion properly!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Let's do it!
Shirone and Neid jumped to their feet and cheered.
The capital of the Tormia Kingdom, Vashka.
Deep within the underground mountain range surrounding the royal palace lay a dungeon, 70 meters below ground, completed a century ago.
Known as the Labyrinth of Darkness, this place had once been the hideout of the great archmage Viltor Akein, who once ruled the world.
Akein, an authority on dark magic, had clashed with numerous nations before vanishing without a trace 40 years ago due to a certain incident.
At the time, Akein's mage rank was an unofficial 3rd class.
Once a mage reached 3rd class or higher, regardless of official recognition, they were granted the title of 'Great Archmage.'
Mage ranks were determined by achievements and fame, managed by the Magic Association through a special scoring system.
To rise from 10th to 9th class required 1,000 points combined from achievements and fame.
But to go from 4th to 3rd class required a staggering 280 million points.
In other words, a 3rd-class mage was someone who had amassed unimaginable achievements and fame.
For comparison, clearing a danger level A dungeon awarded 500 points, while receiving an invitation to the royal palace and an audience with the king awarded 100 points in fame.
This gave a glimpse of how much Akein had shaken the world.
Akein had reached the rank of unofficial 3rd-class mage at the age of 89, 40 years ago.
At the time, his score was 100 million in achievements and 180 million in fame.
His fame outweighed his achievements because he'd been a troublemaker who turned the continent upside down.
Even now, elderly men in their seventies shuddered at the mere mention of Akein's name—a testament to his infamy, even without the 280 million points.
Huff. Huff.
Inside a crystal tube filled with green liquid, Akein opened his eyes.
Naked, his skin sagged with the wear of 149 years.

blocks.speaker: Akein
blocks.content: 'At last… at last, the essence of darkness is complete.'
It was the power of darkness, gathered over 40 years.

blocks.speaker: Akein
blocks.content: Akein planned to absorb it and reclaim his former glory.
Humans aged and died because their cells reached the limit of self-replication.
But Akein had suppressed cell division using a life-support device.
His gleaming eyes turned to the crystal on the desk.
Once shining like a diamond, it was now pitch black.
This was the result of injecting it daily with dark magic to accumulate power.

blocks.speaker: Akein
blocks.content: 'Alpheas, wait for me! I will repay this humiliation.'
After suffering a fatal wound from Alpheas in his early twenties, Akein's magical power had sharply declined.

blocks.speaker: Akein
blocks.content: But clinging to vengeance, he had survived.
And today, he would regain his former power, rising once more as a great archmage.
As the green liquid drained from the life-support device, Akein's back hunched further.
His body was so decrepit it seemed ready for a coffin.
The front of the crystal tube split open, and Akein crawled out, gasping for breath.

blocks.speaker: Akein
blocks.content: Canis! Arin! Are you here?
A boy and girl who looked youthful entered.
Their gloomy aura betrayed their mastery of dark magic.

blocks.speaker: Boy
blocks.content: You called, Master.

blocks.speaker: Akein
blocks.content: Clothes. Bring me my clothes.
